revm-hardhat-examples.description: Working Hardhat and dApp examples for Polkadot Hub, including smart contract deployment and Next.js dApp projects

tasks.title: Zero to Hero Smart Contract DApp
tasks.objective: Set up a Hardhat project, create and deploy a Storage smart contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet, then build a Next.js dApp with Viem that connects a wallet, reads on-chain data, and writes transactions.

tokens: Test PAS tokens from the Polkadot faucet (https://faucet.polkadot.io/)
wallet: MetaMask or compatible Ethereum wallet with an exported private key

env_vars.name: PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: Wallet private key for signing contract deployment transactions. Export from MetaMask or another Ethereum-compatible wallet.

steps.description: This command is interactive. When prompted, select 'Create a TypeScript project' and accept all default options. If running non-interactively, you may need to pipe input or use flags to bypass prompts.

Delete any default contract files in the contracts/ directory, then fetch the reference file and save it as contracts/Storage.sol. This contract stores a uint256, emits a NumberStored event on update, and exposes a public storedNumber getter.

steps.action: Configure Hardhat for Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description: Replace the generated hardhat.config.ts entirely with the fetched reference file. It includes the polkadotTestNet network configuration using the RPC URL and process.env.PRIVATE_KEY. Also create a .env file in the storage-contract directory with PRIVATE_KEY=<your-private-key>.

steps.action: Create the Hardhat Ignition deployment module
steps.description: Delete any default files in the ignition/modules/ directory, then fetch the reference file and save it as ignition/modules/Storage.ts. It defines a StorageModule that deploys the Storage contract.

steps.action: Deploy the contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description: This command is interactive — it will prompt 'Confirm deploy to network polkadotTestNet (420420417)?' and you must respond 'yes'. Save the deployed contract address from the output (e.g., StorageModule#Storage - 0x...) — you will need it in step 11.
steps.expected_output: [ StorageModule ] successfully deployed. Deployed Addresses: StorageModule#Storage - 0x...

steps.description: Navigate back to the project root (polkadot-hub-tutorial) before running create-next-app.

Fetch the reference file and save it as viem.ts in the dapp project root (dapp/viem.ts, not inside app/). This file defines the Polkadot Hub TestNet chain, creates a public client for reading data, and provides a getWalletClient function for signing transactions. Other files import from this path.

steps.action: Copy the contract ABI to the dApp
steps.description: Copy the ABI JSON generated by Hardhat compilation into the dApp's abis directory so the frontend can import it.

Fetch the reference file and save it as utils/contract.ts (at dapp/utils/contract.ts). Important: the reference file contains a hardcoded contract address. You must replace that address with the actual contract address you saved from the deployment output in step 7.

Fetch the reference file and save it as app/components/WalletConnect.tsx. This component handles MetaMask wallet connection, network switching to Polkadot Hub TestNet, and displays the connected account.

Fetch the reference file and save it as app/components/ReadContract.tsx. This component reads the storedNumber value from the contract using viem's readContract and polls for updates every 10 seconds.

Fetch the reference file and save it as app/components/WriteContract.tsx. This component allows users to input a number and send a setNumber transaction, with simulation, wallet signing, and receipt confirmation.

steps.action: Update the main page to integrate all components
steps.description: Fetch the reference file and replace the generated app/page.tsx. This file imports and renders WalletConnect, ReadContract, and WriteContract components with shared account state.

steps.expected_output: The dApp is available at http://localhost:3000 with a wallet connection button, stored number display, and update form.

files.description: Solidity contract that stores a uint256, emits NumberStored event on update, and exposes a public storedNumber getter
Hardhat configuration with Polkadot Hub TestNet network settings (chain ID 420420417)
Hardhat Ignition deployment module for the Storage contract
Viem client setup with Polkadot Hub TestNet chain config, public client, and wallet client factory
Contract address, ABI import, and helper functions to get read-only and signer contract instances
Contract ABI JSON copied from Hardhat build artifacts
React component handling MetaMask connection, account tracking, and Polkadot Hub TestNet network switching
React component that reads storedNumber from the contract and polls for updates
React component with form input to call setNumber, including simulation, signing, and receipt handling
Main Next.js page composing WalletConnect, ReadContract, and WriteContract with shared account state

error_patterns.cause: The wallet does not have enough test PAS tokens to cover gas fees for deployment or transactions.
error_patterns.resolution: Request test tokens from the Polkadot faucet at https://faucet.polkadot.io/ and ensure the correct account is funded.

error_patterns.pattern: HardhatError: HH108: Cannot connect to the network
error_patterns.cause: The Polkadot Hub TestNet RPC endpoint is unreachable or the URL is misconfigured.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the RPC URL is https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet and that you have network connectivity.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: No Ethereum browser provider detected
error_patterns.cause: The dApp is running in a browser without MetaMask or a compatible wallet extension installed.
error_patterns.resolution: Install MetaMask browser extension and refresh the page.

error_patterns.cause: Polkadot Hub TestNet has not been added to the user's MetaMask wallet.
error_patterns.resolution: The WalletConnect component handles this automatically by calling wallet_addEthereumChain. If it persists, manually add the network in MetaMask with chain ID 420420417.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Account not found on current network
error_patterns.cause: The wallet is connected to a different network than Polkadot Hub TestNet.
error_patterns.resolution: Switch to Polkadot Hub TestNet in MetaMask or use the 'Switch to Polkadot Testnet' button in the dApp.

error_patterns.pattern: PRIVATE_KEY is not set or is empty
error_patterns.cause: The .env file is missing or the PRIVATE_KEY variable is not set.
error_patterns.resolution: Create a .env file in the storage-contract directory with PRIVATE_KEY=<your-private-key>. Export your private key from MetaMask under Account Details.

supplementary_context.description: These resolved documentation pages provide deeper context on wallet setup, network configuration, and smart contract fundamentals. Consult them if you need to understand why a step works, not just how to execute it.

pages.relevance: Network connection details, chain IDs, RPC URLs, and wallet configuration for Polkadot Hub TestNet and MainNet
How to obtain test PAS tokens needed for gas fees during contract deployment and transactions
Detailed walkthrough of Hardhat project setup and basic contract deployment, useful if the agent needs more context on Hardhat configuration
